=== #9880 signed iOS device build — proof ===
date: 2026-06-28T06:09:09Z
device: MoonCycles  iPhone 16 Pro Max (iPhone17,2)  iOS 18.7.8  udid 59EBB356-BC44-5AA2-91F1-E6AAE756BB86

Two signed device builds were produced, installed, and launched on the physical
iPhone, each with the WakeController (source-aliased @elizaos/ui) and the Swabble
native wake-detector framework bundled:

1) cloud thin-client  (build:ios:cloud:device, APP_STORE_LOCAL_RUNTIME=0)
2) local on-device runtime (build:ios:local:device + signing, FULL_BUN_ENGINE=1)

--- xcodebuild ---
both: ** BUILD SUCCEEDED **

--- codesign -dvvv App.app (local-runtime variant) ---
Identifier=ai.elizaos.app
Authority=Apple Development: Shaw Walters (UT5K5Q5EVF)
Authority=Apple Worldwide Developer Relations Certification Authority
Authority=Apple Root CA
TeamIdentifier=25877RY2EH
Sealed Resources version=2 rules=10 files=740

--- bundled frameworks (local-runtime variant) ---
/tmp/eliza-ios-dd/Build/Products/Debug-iphoneos/App.app/Frameworks/ElizaBunEngine.framework
/tmp/eliza-ios-dd/Build/Products/Debug-iphoneos/App.app/Frameworks/ElizaosCapacitorSwabble.framework
  ElizaBunEngine.framework      = the on-device agent runtime (runs the agent on the phone)
  ElizaosCapacitorSwabble.framework = the native wake detector (the controller's live swabble-fallback path)

--- devicectl install + launch ---
App installed: bundleID ai.elizaos.app
  cloud variant  -> Bundle/Application/B7D126EC-970B-4AC2-9BAD-D065C0C468C0/App.app/
  local variant  -> Bundle/Application/BFB664CB-E941-475B-A5AA-751E91D5630A/App.app/
Launched on-device (pids 858 / 888 / 907). App shell renders on real hardware
(ios-device-booting.png / ios-device-local-1.png).

--- remaining blocker for the in-app voice UI (NOT a wake-word issue) ---
The app's auth/boot gate polls a backend /api/auth/status which returned
HTTP 503 "Agent is in an error state" (observed over HTTP/3 to a remote host in
device syslog, both variants — the app is configured to a persisted remote
backend that is currently down). Getting past this gate to the live voice
listening UI requires interactive first-run onboarding (selecting a healthy
local/cloud backend) plus a working agent backend — environmental, and
independent of the frontend WakeController change in this PR.

The wake path the device would exercise (openWakeWord head fast-path / two-stage
ASR, Swabble fallback) is covered by the unit + host real-audio tiers; the
shipped hey-eliza head's on-device accuracy is ~98.8% TA / ~3.6% FA (held-out
250+250) per the head-provenance commits.
